title: Dollar General prioritizes in-store audio over digital screens
description: As retailers start to experiment with digital screens to display advertising in stores, Dollar General is taking a more old-school approach.

title: How ThredUp is using AI to improve its marketing operations and move further up the funnel
description: At the Modern Retail Marketing Summit in Huntington Beach, California, ThredUp's SVP and head of marketing, Kristen Brophy, spoke about the company's approach to customer acquisition and, specifically, how ThredUp is trying to improve its forecasting with the help of AI.

title: Digiday+ Research: Marketing workflows benefit from AI, but trust is still a barrier to adoption
description: Research shows that while marketers see AI's benefits, trust and complexity issues are barriers to widespread adoption.

title: Organic Search Winners Share 5 Traits
description:
A just-released study of 400 websites identifies organic-search success across multiple core updates.  
The post Organic Search Winners Share 5 Traits appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Uber Eats launches retail returns
description: The delivery company wants to solve one of the biggest pain points in retail, and is starting with companies like Best Buy and Dick’s Sporting Goods.

title: Home Depot eyes same-day, next-day delivery site in New York
description: The potential distribution center in Yaphank, which the retailer is seeking a tax break for, would be part of its wider supply chain investment strategy.

title: Target to open 6 new stores in May
description: The mass retailer is leaning into larger store footprints, with most of the new locations topping its average square footage.

title: Richard Baker claps back, moves to quash subpoena in Saks Global bankruptcy case
description: The retailer’s former executive chairman said he’s been locked out of his company email and doesn’t have all the details a group of creditors is seeking.

title: QVC Group files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
description:
QVC Group has announced its filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and a restructuring support agreement. The company, which owns television channels QVC and HSN, announced its filing on April 16, 2026. QVC said its bankruptcy filing factored into how it would implement its restructuring agreement, which outlines a plan to “substantially reduce the company’s debt […]
The post QVC Group files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Lululemon launches ecommerce site in Mexico
description:
Apparel brand Lululemon announced its launch of an ecommerce website in Mexico, as of April 20. The retailer’s new ecommerce site “scales its integrated retail and digital presence and omnichannel capabilities in the market,” according to the announcement. The company also said the site complements Lululemon’s plans to increase its physical-store footprint in Mexico during […]
The post Lululemon launches ecommerce site in Mexico appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: ‘We’re not Nespresso’: Inside Green Mountain Coffee Roasters’ new campaign
description: The Keurig Dr Pepper brand is focusing on farm-grown quality in an effort that introduces a green mountain goat as a mascot — not a celebrity ambassador.

title: How SMBs Can Gain An Edge With Agentic AI: Key Trends From Our Marketing Report
description: Our 10th annual State of Marketing Report surveyed 4,450 marketers worldwide — here's what the data said: SMBs can outpace the competition with agentic AI.

title: #210: Stanford 2026 AI Index, OpenAI Internal Shakeups, What Agents Mean for Business, Claude Design & Dwarkesh vs. Jensen
description:
OpenAI is "deeply unfocused," says one of its own early investors. And that's only one of many threads pulling at the company this week.

Paul and Mike dig into OpenAI's reorg, IPO drama, and valuation pushback, Stanford's 2026 AI Index, the real implications of AI agents in the enterprise, Tim Cook's Apple exit, Anthropic's White House thaw, the Dwarkesh-Jensen Huang debate, Claude Design's launch, and rapid-fire updates across Claude Opus 4.7, Cerebras, Harvey Agents, and more.

Show Notes: Access the show notes and show links here

AI Pulse Survey: Fill out this weeks AI Pulse Survey here

Timestamps:

00:00:00 — Intro

00:05:41 — The 2026 AI Index Report

00:19:10 — OpenAI Shifts and Shakeups

00:34:27 — The Business Implications of Agents

00:50:56 — Apple’s Tim Cook Stepping Down

00:54:11 — Anthropic Launches Claude Design, Targets Canva and Figma

01:01:26 — Anthropic's Possible Reconciliation with the White House

01:06:02 — Dwarkesh vs. Jensen

01:15:08 — Gen AI Traffic Share

title: Ad Tech Briefing: The days of bloat are over, as efficiency drives ad tech’s C-suite exodus
description: Faced with increasing competition from LLMs, ad tech players have to better demonstrate their value-add.

title: Why OpenAI is ‘running at lightning speed’ to build an ad business
description: The AI ad wars have officially begun, and OpenAI is staffing up and dropping rates to lure brands away from the likes of Meta and Google.

title: [AINews] Moonshot Kimi K2.6: the world's leading Open Model refreshes to catch up to Opus 4.6 (ahead of DeepSeek v4?)
description: Yay Kimi!!!

title: Chinese tech workers are starting to train their AI doubles–and pushing back
description: Tech workers in China are being instructed by their bosses to train AI agents to replace them—and it’s prompting a wave of soul-searching among otherwise enthusiastic early adopters.  Earlier this month a GitHub project called Colleague Skill, which claimed workers could use it to “distill” their colleagues’ skills and personality traits and replicate them with…

title: Adobe expands agency partnerships as part of agentic AI platform debut
description: Omnicom, Publicis and WPP are among the agencies that are standardizing on the new CX Enterprise platform to co-develop solutions for joint clients.

title: Marketers Need to Stop Chasing Data and Start Harnessing Audience Signals
description: This post was created in partnership with Sprout Social Brands have more data than ever, but most of it remains untapped. What’s missing isn’t more data. It’s the signal that […]

title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
description: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to improve productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

title: 🔬 Training Transformers to solve 95% failure rate of Cancer Trials — Ron Alfa & Daniel Bear, Noetik
description: 95% of cancer treatments fail to pass clinical trials, but it may be a matching problem — that Noetik is solving with autoregressive transformers like TARIO-2!

title: Adobe Launches AI Agent Platform for CX
description: The company is looking to bolster customer service by scaling AI agent workflows, but it's entering a noisy market.

title: Siemens Trials Nvidia-Powered Humanoid
description: The trial, which took place at a Siemens factory in Germany, was pitched as a crucial step towards fully AI-integrated operations.

title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Files for IPO
description: The move comes after the vendor forged significant deals with OpenAI and AWS earlier this year.

title: Physical AI Edges Closer to Real-World Deployments
description: A new report from French IT consulting firm Capgemini found that businesses are rapidly moving physical AI from experimentation to implementation.

title: Chinese Vendor Claims First Large-Scale Embodied AI Deployment
description: Agibot says its project marks a significant shift in embodied AI from pilots to industrial use.

title: How SMBs Can Gain An Edge With Agentic AI: Key Trends From Our Marketing Report
description: Our 10th annual State of Marketing Report surveyed 4,450 marketers worldwide — here's what the data said: SMBs can outpace the competition with agentic AI.
